data.description:
Major sitewide button restyle, three parts:

(1) FROST EFFECT — apply a frosted-glass effect to buttons and navbar buttons. Reference implementation lives in the Renoverse AI repository at ~/Dev/renoverse-ai-website (and possibly ~/Dev/renoverse-site / ~/Dev/renoverse-prototype). Look at their button component definitions and navbar component definitions — port the backdrop-blur + translucent background + subtle border treatment into Fractal's Button primitives. Should work over any house background.

(2) HOUSE-ACCENT DECORATIVE BORDER + MANDELBROTS — the decorative border around buttons and the corner Mandelbrot PNGs should take the house's highlight/accent color (the non-background color on each house page — same color as the Jacquard letter and eyebrow at the top of each page). This makes the buttons pop against the page background. Each house already has a per-house accent palette (see FRAC-37); reuse those tokens. Buttons in shared/sitewide contexts (footer, home) need a sensible default — probably the cream/foreground default.

(3) DEFAULT TEXT COLOR MATCHES PAGE — for now, button text should default to the same color as the body text on that page (whichever of foreground/background the page uses). I.e., if a page is on a dark background with cream text, button text is cream. Don't introduce a separate button-text token yet — just inherit the page's text color.

Acceptance: (a) buttons sitewide have the frost effect; (b) decorative borders + corner Mandelbrots on each house page take that house's accent color; (c) button text matches body text color on each page; (d) navbar buttons get the frost effect too; (e) mobile 375px still legible; (f) verify on Story, NLA, Political Club, Visit, Events, Lab, Campus, Home, Protocol.
data.title: Button overhaul: frost effect + house-accent decorations + page-matched text color

data.body: Plan written. Port Renoverse .btn--frosted recipe (cream-tint rgba(242,234,216,.08) + backdrop-blur-md + accent border + text-current) into Button default variant; accent reaches via single CSS var --btn-accent set on each page <main> root — no new prop, Mandelbrots inherit via currentColor wrapper. Default falls back to currentColor for Home/Footer. One open Q: Navbar has no Buttons — recommend frosting mobile menu overlay; will default to that at impl unless user redirects.

User decision: skip navbar (option 3) — interpret 'navbar buttons get frost' as already satisfied since navbar has no Button instances. Impl focuses on Button/PrimaryButton primitives + per-page --btn-accent CSS var.
Implemented sitewide button frost + accent propagation. PR opened: #205.

Reviewed PR #205 with fresh context. PASS.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (all met):
(a) Frost recipe present in button.tsx default variant: border + bg-[rgba(242,234,216,0.08)] + backdrop-blur-md + [border-color:var(--btn-accent,currentColor)] + text-current + hover:bg-[rgba(242,234,216,0.16)]. Other variants (outline/ghost/link) untouched. MET.
(b) --btn-accent set on <main> of 8 house pages (Campus, Events, Lab, LiberalArts, Neighborhood, People, PoliticalClub, Story) — each maps to that house's deep/accent token. Corner spans now spread accentColorStyle ({ color: 'var(--btn-accent, currentColor)' }) into each of the four corner spans, so MandelbrotIcon's fill='currentColor' resolves to the accent. MET.
(c) text-current on default variant + each page's text-foreground/text-background/text-white cascades through. MET.
(d) Navbar frost — Navbar.tsx untouched per user direction (no Button instances in navbar). Confirmed via git diff. MET.
(e) Mobile-first: padding/sizing unchanged. Class string preserves existing px-8 py-5 chrome. MET.
(f) Home + ProtocolPage NOT touched — fall back to currentColor border (charcoal/cream), correctly matching plan's 'sensible default'. Other 7 pages explicitly handled. MET.

CAMPUS OVERRIDE removed: bg-foreground/20 hover:bg-foreground/30 dropped from PrimaryButton in Campus.tsx — Campus now inherits sitewide frost over green bg. MET.

TESTS:
- All 23 button tests pass, including new accent propagation assertion (--btn-accent on <main> -> Button class contains [border-color:var(--btn-accent,currentColor)]).
- 4 pre-existing failures (footer.test.tsx, navigation.test.tsx, neighborhood.test.tsx) confirmed to exist on origin/master with the same test files — unrelated to FRAC-52.
- pnpm typecheck clean.

CREAM-ON-CREAM JUDGMENT:
Story (cream-bg #D4BA58), Home (cream), Protocol (cream): 8% cream tint nearly invisible, reduces to 'accent border + corners only' chrome. Per plan's documented Risk #2 this is an accepted tradeoff that makes the design self-consistent rather than a regression. Border + corners do the visual work. PASS as-is — no inline tweak needed; any change here (e.g. gating on text color to swap to rgba(0,0,0,0.04)) would complicate the recipe for marginal benefit and was explicitly weighed in the plan.

SKETCHY BITS: none. The accentColorStyle spread approach in corner spans is cleaner than the plan's 'wrap in single span' suggestion (preserves per-corner positioning + rotation). Test asserts the class reference rather than computed style — appropriate workaround for jsdom's lack of CSS var resolution.

Verdict: implementation matches plan with no substantive divergence.
